vitals.aid_stations: Self-supported ultra format; no formal aid stations specified
vitals.field_size: Small field typical for ultra events

course_description.overview: Ultra-endurance road cycling challenge of nearly 650 km in a single push from Bordeaux on the Atlantic Ocean and Garonne River banks to Paris on the Seine River, traversing flat to rolling French landscapes.[6]

key_segments.name: Bordeaux to Châteauroux
key_segments.description: Initial segment through southwestern France, likely flat forests and plains similar to Landes region.

key_segments.name: Châteauroux to Paris
key_segments.description: Northern push through central France to the Eiffel Tower, rolling terrain.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Extreme distance challenge emphasizing endurance over technical difficulty, with long straight roads, subtle hills, and mental fatigue from monotonous flats.[3][6]

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Bordeaux Airport (BOD) for start; Paris airports (CDG, ORY) for finish. Train connections available between cities.
logistics.accommodation: Self-managed; pre/post-event hotels in Bordeaux and Paris.
logistics.registration_details: Register via organizer site; date and price TBD for 2026. Limited spots for ultra events.[3]
logistics.bike_logistics: Fly with bike or ship; standard road bike suitable. No support vehicles mentioned.
logistics.local_considerations: Spring weather variable; watch for traffic on roads. France has good cycling infrastructure but event is on open roads.

final_verdict.one_liner: Brutal flat ultra-road test of pure endurance from wine country to the City of Light.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Hardcore ultra-cyclists seeking a single-push 650km road sufferfest without gravel or climbs.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes for ultra-distance purists chasing iconic France traversal.
pro_tips: Train for 20+ hour saddle time on trainer with headwind simulation.
Pack lights and spares; self-supported format demands reliability.
Start early to beat summer heat; monitor Loire Valley winds.
Celebrate finish with Eiffel Tower views and French recovery meal.
